4 Quality Information Document for OMI_CIRCULATION_VOLTRANS_IBI_section_integrated_anomalies 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-IBI-OMI-QUID-CIRCULATION-
VOLTRANS-section-integrated-anomalies 
Issue 1.1 – March 2026 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
 
OMI_CIRCULATION_VOLTRANS_IBI_section_integrated_anomalies. 
 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
This OMI provides estimates of integrated volume transport across a set of vertical 
sections defined at various locations within the IBI domain. The selection of vertical 
sections is aimed at monitoring the temporal variability of four characteristic ocean  
currents in the IBI area: water transport towards Scandinavian seas through Rockall 
Trough (Holliday et al., 2008; Lozier and Stewart, 2009), the Algerian Current (Tintoré et 
al, 1988; Benzohra and Millot, 1995; Font et al., 1998), the Canary Current (Kno ll et al. 
2002, Mason et al. 2011), and the Azores Current (Mason et al., 2011). This product is 
calculated from an ensemble of Copernicus Marine products, thus providing results on 
transport variability (ensemble mean) as well as uncertainties in the estimates 
(ensemble standard deviation). 
 
The lateral and vertical boundaries of these sections were defined according to the 
results of the analysis conducted by de Pascual -Collar et al., 2024. Thus , the lateral 
boundaries were determined based on the analysis of the average transport in each 
section, while the vertical boundaries were defined according to the typical density 
values of the water mass being monitored. 
 
Table 1 shows the geographical boundaries (minimum and maximum latitude and 
longitude) used for the calculation of transports associated with each current. As 
explained in Pascual -Collar et al. (2024), the vertical limits of the monitoring sections 
were de fined dynamically based on the characteristic density values of each 
monitored water mass (Density column in Table 1).

for surface, sfl for seafloor).

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document describes OMI_CIRCULATION_VOLTRANS_IBI_section_integrated_anomalies, detailing its computation method and the Copernicus Marine products used as input.
